actionable_feedback: Produce an easily-parsable export of the spreadsheet's key computed tables and formula cells (CSV or plain-text 'FORMULAS' worksheet) so reviewers can validate formulas without needing binary XLSX parsing.
Ensure the workbook is saved in a format and encoding that standard libraries (openpyxl/pandas) can parse; if manual OOXML emission is used, include a text-export alongside it.
Add a clearly visible cell (and reference it in the memo) that contains the numeric value used for cost_proxy_per_record_usd and document the rationale for the chosen value.
Document and justify any heuristic constants used (e.g., the 0.25 multiplier in weibull_weight) in the memo and point to the exact workbook cell(s) where the constant is applied.

In the memo, link each recommendation to specific workbook cell ranges (sheet and cell addresses) and include a brief table showing the +/-5% perturbation results for the technical_risk for each record.

Optionally include a short, human-readable worksheet listing the core formulas (as text) and a sample of computed numeric outputs for R1-R4 to make semantic checks reproducible even in constrained environments.

feedback_summary: Required deliverables are present and deterministic artifact-contract checks passed. However the grader/verifier could not programmatically parse the XLSX (openpyxl unavailable) so executable formulas and numeric computations could not be independently verified. The memo documents methods and results but omits an explicit numeric value for cost_proxy_per_record_usd and does not fully justify the weibull weighting constant (0.25). These omissions reduced grader confidence in the economic ranking and led to partial semantic credit.

recommended_next_steps: Add a 'FORMULAS' worksheet with textual formula mappings and key parameter cell locations.
Add a 'EXPORT' CSV containing computed numeric columns for R1..R4 and the +/-5% perturbation results.
Update the memo to include explicit cost_proxy_per_record_usd and justification plus cell references for constants.
